steps.action: Halay is the national dance of Turkey with pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat.
steps.text: Halay is the national dance of Turkey with pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat.

steps.action: It refers to all traditional circular and line d
steps.text: It refers to all traditional circular and line dances performed across the country.

steps.action: Circular and line dances in Turkey are commonly
steps.text: Circular and line dances in Turkey are commonly performed at weddings and festive gatherings, traditionally accompanied by the zurna and davul, or sometimes by the singing of the dancers themselves.

steps.action: In recent years, electronic instruments have inc
steps.text: In recent years, electronic instruments have increasingly been used in place of traditional musical instruments.

steps.action: Halay dancers typically form a circle or a line,
steps.text: Halay dancers typically form a circle or a line, holding each other by the fingers, hands, or shoulders.

steps.action: The first and last dancers may also hold a handk
steps.text: The first and last dancers may also hold a handkerchief known as a mendil.

steps.action: These dances usually begin slowly and gradually
steps.text: These dances usually begin slowly and gradually speed up in tempo, and their name, style, and musical characteristics vary from one town or village to another.

steps.action: The name of the dance was taken from the refrain
steps.text: The name of the dance was taken from the refrain of a Turkish folk song popular among the community that accompanied the dance, suggesting that these Pontic Greeks were Turkish-speaking.

steps.action: It was a slow-paced, semi-circular dance, charac
steps.text: It was a slow-paced, semi-circular dance, characterized by intense bending movements and rhythmic foot strikes.

steps.action: Dancers would start with their right foot, takin
steps.text: Dancers would start with their right foot, taking the first three steps diagonally to the right.

steps.action: On the fourth step, they would extend their feet
steps.text: On the fourth step, they would extend their feet toward the center of the circle, then step backward with the left foot on the fifth step, placing the toe inward and the heel outward.

steps.action: They would return to the position of the fourth
steps.text: They would return to the position of the fourth step on the sixth step, then repeat the sequence, moving in unison to the accompaniment of musical instruments, such as the violin, oud, zurna and davul.

steps.action: The dance soon became widely recognized among ot
steps.text: The dance soon became widely recognized among other communities in Cappadocia, who began using the term to refer to all circular and line folk dances throughout the country.

steps.action: However, it is being revived and taught in dance
steps.text: However, it is being revived and taught in dance schools.

steps.action: Yallı is danced in some parties but majority of
steps.text: Yallı is danced in some parties but majority of weddings perform it, with the people forming a circle around the Groom and Bride.

steps.action: Yallı is a part of Azerbaijani music and dance,
steps.text: Yallı is a part of Azerbaijani music and dance, and teaching and it goes back to 1932.

steps.action: Yallı is an important dance for the Azerbaijani
steps.text: Yallı is an important dance for the Azerbaijani people and ethnic groups residing in Azerbaijan.

steps.action: The term is used among Turks, Kurds, Araps, and
steps.text: The term is used among Turks, Kurds, Araps, and Asia Minor Greeks (particularly Pontic Greeks, Karamanlides, and Cappadocian Greeks).

steps.action: The historical documentation of halay dates back
steps.text: The historical documentation of halay dates back to the early 20th century.

steps.action: In Azerbaijan, Yalli goes by region, and is most
steps.text: In Azerbaijan, Yalli goes by region, and is most commonly danced in Nakhchivan and by the Yeraz community of Armenia.

steps.action: In Nakhchivan, it is accompanied by Zurna and Da
steps.text: In Nakhchivan, it is accompanied by Zurna and Davul, but anywhere else in Azerbaijan, it is most commonly danced with Nağara (Caucasian dhol) and Zurna.

steps.action: Most of them take the name of the region it is d
steps.text: Most of them take the name of the region it is danced in, such as Quba Yallısı, Şərur Yallısı, and Qarabağ Yallısı.

steps.action: However, there are ones that are not named by th
steps.text: However, there are ones that are not named by the region such as Günel Yallısı or Köçəri.

steps.action: In Azerbaijan itself without Nakhchivan, Yallı i
steps.text: In Azerbaijan itself without Nakhchivan, Yallı is either forgotten or not danced completely.
